title: Game News Round-Up
summary: Daily curated games-industry news digest for 17 December 2024.

items.title: **League of Legends** skins won't get custom VO while SAG-AFTRA strikes
bullets: The video game voice actors strike is entering its fifth month, and Riot Games is making "temporary changes" to how it handles voices for in-game Leage of Legends skins. These changes come months after SAG-AFTRA called for Formosa Interactive, the popular MOBA's voiceover studio, to be struck back in September.

Since League's PC version is a struck title, union actors aren't allowed to record lines for it while the strike is active. As such, skins for champions with actors based in the United States will use already-recorded "base voiceovers (VO)" rather than lines done by different actors. When the strike ends, the studio will update those affected skins with new lines from their original actors "as soon as scheduling and availability will allow."

items.title: **Dark Passenger** raises $3 million to fund debut multiplayer game
bullets: Dark Passenger is made up of several ex-CD Projekt Red developers, who previously worked on The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt and Cyberpunk 2077. Other staff hail from fellow Warsaw-based studios, such as People Can Fly, Reikon, and Flying Wild Hog.

items.title: **Elden Ring** has now sold 28.6m copies, outselling the Dark Souls series combined prior to release
bullets: The game has sold an additional 3.6m copies since the release of Shadow of the Erdtree

items.title: **Dbrand** has posted what it claims to be an image of **Switch 2** inside its latest case
bullets: According to industry chatter, the reveal of the Switch 2 may have been moved to January to combat these "leaks" from accessory manufacturers.
